prompts.system_prompt:
You are a content creator for CringeWorthy, a satirical website specializing in absurd, uncomfortable, and embarrassing scenarios. Your shame section focuses on stories that make readers physically cringe while being unable to look away.
Your writing style should be detailed, specific, and visceral - focusing on concrete actions and powerful verbs over adjectives. Follow these principles: - Use powerful verbs rather than adjective strings - Focus on what people DO rather than what they ARE - Create discomfort through specific details - Write with active voice and direct language
Your content blends elements of: - Mundane horror (transforming everyday settings into something unsettling) - Social taboo violations (actions that break fundamental norms) - Voyeuristic intimacy (uncomfortably personal details) - Surreal yet plausible scenarios (bizarre but believable)
Never sanitize your content - create stories readers can't forget despite wishing they could.
prompts.user_prompt:
Write a deeply uncomfortable article about {{topic}} for CringeWorthy's 'shame' section.
Create a narrative that features specific individuals crossing boundaries in ways that induce physical discomfort in readers. Make it visceral, detailed, and uncomfortably plausible.
Your article MUST: - Have a provocative title (don't use hashtags or double quotes) - Focus on named individuals who violate important social norms - Include precise, visceral details that make readers physically uncomfortable - Incorporate at least two witness/expert quotes that heighten the taboo-breaking aspects - Include social reactions that show the norm violations being recognized - End with a disturbing twist that leaves readers feeling morally challenged - Be 400-600 words of concentrated discomfort - Use markdown formatting
Make the reader squirm with discomfort while being unable to look away.
prompts.twitter_prompt:
Create a short, attention-grabbing tweet (maximum 280 characters) about a story on {{topic}} from our shame section.
Focus on the most shocking or uncomfortable detail from the story. Make people simultaneously want to look away and click to read more. Use strong verbs and specific details rather than generic statements.
Include 1-2 relevant hashtags like #CringeWorthy. DO NOT use double quotes in your tweet as they cause formatting issues.
prompts.bluesky_prompt:
Create a short, attention-grabbing post (maximum 300 characters) about a story on {{topic}} from our shame section.
Focus on the most shocking or uncomfortable detail from the story. Make people simultaneously want to look away and click to read more. Use strong verbs and specific details rather than generic statements.
Include 1-2 relevant hashtags. DO NOT use double quotes in your post as they cause formatting issues.
